title: Gärtner/ Gartenhelfer (m/w/d)
description_text:

title: Junior Hulpverlener (24-28 uur) (m/v/x)
description_text:
Stichting 113 Zelfmoordpreventie is de nationale organisatie voor suïcidepreventie. Onze missie is een land waarin niemand eenzaam en radeloos sterft door zelfdoding. Wij zijn ervan overtuigd dat er jaarlijks suïcides voorkomen kunnen worden als het onderwerp beter bespreekbaar wordt en de kennis rondom suïcidaliteit toeneemt. Naast de hulplijn, waar mensen met suïcidale gedachten 24/7 anoniem kunnen bellen of chatten, ontwikkelen we onder andere via de Landelijke Agenda Suïcidepreventie nieuwe interventies waarmee suïcides kunnen worden voorkomen. Onze missie realiseren we met bevlogen (zorg)professionals, vrijwilligers, stagiairs vanuit ons kantoor in Amsterdam Zuidoost.

Taken

De functie

In de rol van Junior Hulpverlener maak je onderdeel uit van het team Hulpverlening en werk je vanuit ons kantoor in Amsterdam. Samen met jouw directe collega’s verricht je de volgende werkzaamheden:

  • Je verleent hulp aan mensen die contact met ons opnemen via telefoon en chat. Tijdens deze gesprekken maak je doelbewust gebruik van de Motiverende Gespreksvoering als methodiek om adequaat om te gaan met suïcidale gedachten. Dit vereist een toewijding met deze gesprekstechniek en de vaardigheid om deze toe te passen met een warm hart en een koel hoofd;
  • Het verrichten van triage werkzaamheden; beoordelen van binnenkomende chats, in verschillende categorieën verdelen naar ernst van de situatie;
  • Het adviseren over en motiveren van cliënten voor het volgen van chattherapie of het zoeken van aanvullende hulpverlening of nemen van vervolgstappen.
  • Jouw persoonlijke ambitie is om het leven van hulpvragers veiliger en minder uitzichtloos te maken. Jij zorgt ervoor dat mensen met zelfmoordgedachten op ons kunnen blijven rekenen. Door bereikbaar te zijn en online, adequate hulp te bieden.

Wat is verder nog belangrijk?

  • Ervaringsdeskundigen zijn altijd welkom waarbij wel verwacht wordt dat je 2 jaar vrij bent van suïcidale klachten;
  • Je bent bereid een driedaagse training Motiverende Gespreksvoering te volgen (en inwerkdiensten);
  • Je bent stressbestendig en kunt goed en effectief in teamverband functioneren;
  • Je bent in staat om op je eigen handelen te reflecteren en bent in staat om feedback te geven en te ontvangen;
  • Je beschikt over beheersing van de Engelse taal is zowel woord als geschrift;
  • Je bent minimaal 24 uur per week beschikbaar (minder uren is niet mogelijk);
  • Wij geloven in groei en ontwikkeling en hebben daarom als uitgangspunt dat junioren na minimaal 7 maanden (tot maximaal twee jaar) doorgroeien naar de rol Hulpverlener Suïcidepreventie (als Hulpverlener Suïcidepreventie ben je tevens bedrijfshulpverlener (BHV'er). Dit houdt in dat wanneer je doorgroeit, je een BHV-opleiding zult volgen);
  • Je bent flexibel inzetbaar ten aanzien van avond-, nacht- en week, weekenddiensten en feestdagen (alle diensten vinden vanuit ons kantoor plaats) waarbij de nadruk ligt op de avonddienst.

Vereisten

Wat is vereist?

  • Je hebt een warm hart en koel hoofd
  • Je beschikt over een afgeronde hbo opleiding (of je staat op het punt deze binnen 2 jaar af te ronden).
  • Je bent gedreven om je volledig in te zetten voor onze missie
  • Je beschikt over uitstekende beheersing van de Nederlandse taal in woord en geschrift
  • Je bent flexibel inzetbaar ten aanzien van avond-, nacht- en week, weekenddiensten (100% vanaf ons kantoor in Amsterdam).

Benefits

Wat krijg je?

  • Arbeidsovereenkomst voor een jaar, met uitzicht op verlenging
  • Salaris conform de cao GGZ schaal 45: €3.088,- tot € 4.149,- o.b.v. 36 uur. Inschaling vindt plaats op basis van het aantal jaren relevante werkervaring.
  • Je werkt in deze functie geregeld buiten kantoortijden. Conform cao GGZ ontvang je hiervoor een aantrekkelijke en substantiële onregelmatigheidstoeslag bovenop je salaris
  • Vakantiegeld (8,33%) en een eindejaarsuitkering (8,33%)
  • 100% vergoeding van je reiskosten wanneer je met het OV reist en/of een reiskostenvergoeding van €0,23 per km woonwerk wanneer je met de auto komt
  • Pensioenregeling bij Pensioenfonds Zorg & Welzijn (PFZW)

Enthousiast geworden?

Dan komen wij graag met jou in contact! Solliciteren kan via onderstaande button.

Voor inhoudelijke vragen over de functie kun je contact opnemen met de Teamleiders van Hulpverlening via ons telefoonnummer: 020 - 3113883. Wil je meer weten over de sollicitatieprocedure?  Neem dan vooral contact op met onze Recruiter Michelle via telefonisch via 020 - 3113883.

Het maken van een assessment behoort tot onze sollicitatieprocedure.

Wij werken niet met een sluitingsdatum voor deze vacature: je hoort snel van ons nadat je hebt gereageerd.

We geloven in de kracht van een inclusieve organisatiecultuur en vinden het belangrijk dat iedereen zichzelf kan zijn. Of het nu gaat om leeftijd, gender, geloof, seksuele oriëntatie, culturele achtergrond, opleiding, neurodiversiteit, zichtbare of niet-zichtbare beperkingen, etc.: iedereen is welkom!

Voor het werken bij 113 is een VOG (Verklaring Omtrent Gedrag) vereist.

Michelle Vos
Corporate Recruiter

title: Ausbildung zum Sport- & Fitnesskaufmann (m/w/d) ODER zum Sport- & Gesundheitstrainer (m/w/d)

title: Learning & Development Consultant
description_text:
About the Role:
  
As a CBRE Learning and Development Consultant, you will assist in managing the development, implementation, and administration of scalable learning and development programs that support business objectives. You will partner with stakeholders to identify needs, recommend solutions, and drive learning initiatives that enhance employee capability, performance, and engagement.
  
What You’ll Do:
  
· Consults with business units and subject matter experts to identify learning and performance needs, recommend appropriate solutions and guide content development efforts.
  
· Designs and administers training programs (virtual and live instructor led trainings, digital learning resources, videos, eLearnings, and microlearnings) and recommends delivery approaches that best support learning outcomes and business needs.
  
· Applies learning and instructional design expertise to address complex business challenges and develop innovative learning solutions.
  
· Coordinates, designs and delivers virtual and in-person instructor-led training across the US.
  
· Creates and applies evaluation and assessment tools to measure learning effectiveness, impact and alignment with business goals.
  
· Translates complex concepts into clear, practical learning solutions that meet the needs of diverse audiences and support learning and performance goals.
  
· Review training metrics, feedback and evaluation data to identify trends and drive continuous improvement of learning solutions.
  
· Manages communication and marketing plans for new courses and training programs.
  
· Remain current on learning industry trends, methodologies, and best practices and apply them effectively to program design and delivery.
  
· Develops strong relationships and solicit and incorporate feedback from business partners, operations, managers, consultants and trainers.
  
· Lead by example and model behaviors that are consistent with CBRE RISE values.
  
· Contribute to team success through effective communication and collaboration with teammates and partner team members.
  
What You’ll Need:
  
· Bachelor's Degree preferred with 5-8 years of experience in learning and development roles, training, instructional design or related roles. In lieu of a degree, a combination of experience and education will be considered.
  
· Experience delivering and facilitating training in-person and/or virtually.
  
· Ability to work autonomously within broad guidelines and adapt to evolving business needs and priorities.
  
· Ability to exercise judgment based on the analysis of multiple sources of information.
  
· Willingness to take a new perspective on existing solutions.

title: Proposal Strategist - Management & Support (South Central Region)
description_text:
**Description**
  
 
  
Burns & McDonnell is seeking a Proposal Strategist to support pursuit development in Houston, Texas. This role is responsible for leading the development of proposals from initial Request for Proposal/Information/Qualifications (RFP/RFI/RFQ) from pre-positioning through submission and shortlist interview, when applicable.
  
 
  

  
 
  
The Proposal Strategist partners with business development and project management leadership to develop distinctive value propositions that strengthen our competitive position, win work, and build lasting client relationships. This individual is a resourceful, collaborative professional who thrives in a fast-paced environment and is motivated to continuously improve processes and outcomes.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Key Responsibilities**
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Strategy & Planning**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Support sales teams in pre-positioning efforts by shaping strategy, messaging, and capture plans ahead of RFP release.
  
 
  
+ Lead the development of qualifications packages and responses by collaborating with sales and technical teams to tailor content, highlight relevant experience, and align messaging with client priorities
  
 
  
+ Develop and lead proposal and opportunity management plans, including drafting storyboards, defining win strategies, and delegating pursuit team assignments.
  
 
  
+ Facilitate go/no-go decisions, proposal kickoff meetings, and ongoing team alignment discussions.
  
 
  
+ Drive collaboration across technical, business development, and marketing teams to shape proposal direction and messaging.

+ Write and develop client-focused proposal content, including win themes, cover letters, executive summaries, project descriptions, and resumes.
  
 
  
+ Incorporate company standards and tailor messaging to align with client needs and evaluation criteria.
  
 
  
+ Utilize AI-based tools, where appropriate, to support drafting, editing, and enhancing proposal content.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Visual Communication & Design**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Translate complex technical information into clear, compelling graphics and visual content.
  
 
  
+ Develop proposal layouts in Microsoft Word and Adobe InDesign, complying with Burns & McDonnell brand standards.
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Proactively lead the creation of client-facing presentations, shaping messaging, coaching presenters, and ensuring alignment with win strategy for account meetings.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Review & Quality Management**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Lead color team reviews with a focus on client benefits and win strategy.
  
 
  
+ Incorporate feedback and refine materials to improve clarity, quality, and competitiveness.
  
 
  
+ Uphold best practices related to content management, quality control, collaboration, and file organization.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Production & Delivery**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Manage proposal schedules and ensure timely delivery of compliant, high-quality submissions.
  
 
  
+ Package and submit proposals in accordance with client requirements, including coordination of digital submissions and hard copy production when required.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Post-Submittal & Business Development Support**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Support shortlist interview preparation, including coaching teams and developing presentation materials.
  
 
  
+ Facilitate debriefs and capture lessons learned to strengthen future pursuits.
  
 
  
+ Assist with submissions for industry awards (e.g., ACEC, ENR) and trade publications.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Events & Marketing Support**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Support conferences and trade shows by coordinating logistics, securing sponsorships, and preparing presentation and thought leadership materials.
  
 
  
+ Occasionally assist with booth setup and on-site event support.
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Perform other duties as assigned.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Qualifications**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Bachelor’s degree in marketing, communications, journalism, business administration, or related field.
  
 
  
+ Applicable years of experience may be substituted for the degree requirement.
  
 
  
+ Minimum 4 years of experience in marketing, business development, or sales within a professional services firm; A/E/C industry experience preferred.
  
 
  
+ Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to translate complex ideas into clear, compelling messaging.
  
 
  
+ Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite required.
  
 
  
+ Proficiency with Adobe Creative Suite (including InDesign) strongly preferred.
  
 
  
+ Experience using AI-based tools for content development is preferred, with the ability to adapt to new technologies.
  
 
  
+ APMP certification (or equivalent) preferred.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Key Attributes**

title: EVP, Treasury Management Product
description_text:
Why Bank OZK
  

  

  
 Founded on a legacy of more than 120 years in banking, Bank OZK is much more than just a company. We’re nationally recognized as an industry leader in financial services. That means we combine exceptional service with innovative technologies to deliver smart solutions to our clients across the country. We’re investing in small businesses, fueling economies in local communities and changing skylines in the largest cities across America. Here, we're not simply filling roles. We're fostering even greater careers. 
  

  
 
  

  
 The foundation for a great career starts with an exceptional team and a comprehensive benefits package. We believe in providing our dedicated team members with the best resources to support their physical, mental and financial wellbeing, including generous PTO, 401(k) matching, health, dental, vision (and pet!) insurance as well as special perks and discounts. Learn more about Bank OZK benefits (https://careers.ozk.com/benefits) . 
  

  
 
  
 
  
Job Purpose & Scope
  

  

  
 The EVP, Treasury Management Product is responsible for end‑to‑end product ownership of Bank OZK’s treasury management receivables and payables solutions. This role owns the full product lifecycle across strategy, design, delivery, and ongoing performance—ensuring solutions are scalable, compliant, client‑centric, and aligned with Bank OZK’s treasury management and payments strategy. 
  

  
 
  

  
 The role serves as the primary product leader for treasury management flows, acting as a cross‑functional coordinator across Technology, Operations, Sales, Finance, Risk, Compliance, and Legal within established governance frameworks. 
  
 
  
Essential Job Functions
  

  

  
 
  

  

  
+  Leads the product strategy, roadmap, and lifecycle management for treasury management receivables and payables solutions 
  

  
+  Develops and maintains a unified vision for end‑to‑end treasury workflows, spanning inbound and outbound funds movement 
  

  
+  Translates market dynamics, client needs, and internal priorities into clear product direction and execution plans 
  

  
+  Owns product strategy and ongoing performance for receivables and collections solutions 
  

  
+  Partners with Commercial Banking, Treasury Sales, and clients to understand receivables use cases and workflows 
  

  
+  Develops business cases for receivables enhancements, pricing strategies, and technology investments 
  

  
+  Oversees delivery and operational readiness of receivables products, ensuring scalability and service quality 
  

  
+  Owns product strategy and lifecycle management for payables and disbursement solutions 
  

  
+  Collaborates with clients and Treasury Sales to understand payables workflows, automation needs, and market expectations 
  

  
+  Develops business cases for payables product enhancements, platform investments, and integrations 
  

  
+  Oversees delivery and operational performance of payables solutions, including onboarding, processing, and service support 
  

  
+  Manages staff to include staffing, assigning and delegating work, training, mentoring, and coaching. Provides technical guidance, conducts performance reviews, and fosters a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. 
  

  
+  Acts as a central product owner coordinating with Technology, Operations, Sales, Finance, Risk, Compliance, and Legal 
  

  
+  Balances client experience, operational capacity, and risk considerations in product decisions 
  

  
+  Ensures all treasury management products meet regulatory, compliance, and internal risk management requirements 
  

  
+  Prepares and presents product updates, business cases, and reviews to senior leadership and internal governance forums 
  

  
+  Monitors product adoption, operational metrics, client feedback, and financial performance 
  

  
+  Identifies opportunities for modernization, simplification, and cost‑to‑serve optimization 
  

  
+  Serves as the voice of the customer in product prioritization and design decisions 
  

  
+  Supports enterprise initiatives related to payments modernization and vendor strategy 
  

  
 
  
Knowledge, Skills & Abilities
  

  

  

  
+  Strong product leadership and cross‑functional collaboration skills 
  

  
+  Ability to manage multiple treasury sub‑domains within a unified product strategy 
  

  
+  Client‑centric mindset with strong understanding of treasury workflows 
  

  
+  Solid analytical, financial, and business case development skills 
  

  
+  Ability to operate effectively in a regulated banking environment 
  

  
+  Ability to demonstrate leadership skills and manage the work performance of others. 
  

  
 
  
Basic Qualifications
  

  

  

  
+  Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, Information Systems, or related field required; MBA preferred 
  

  
+  Minimum of (10) years of experience in commercial banking, treasury management, or payments 
  

  
+  Minimum of five (5) years of experience in treasury management, payments, or product management roles 
  

  
+  Minimum of (2) years of experience managing, leading, or supervising others, required. 
  

  
+  Demonstrated experience with B2B receivables and payables workflows 
  

  
+  Familiarity with regulatory, compliance, and risk frameworks applicable to treasury management products 
  

  
 
  
Job Expectations
  

  

  
 O perate customary equipment and technology used in a business environment, with or without accommodation.

title: Analyst/Sr Analyst, Industrial Engineer (JFK)
description_text:
**Intro**
  

  
Are you ready to explore a world of possibilities, both at work and during your time off? Join our American Airlines family, and you’ll travel the world, grow your expertise and become the best version of you. As you embark on a new journey, you’ll tackle challenges with flexibility and grace, learning new skills and advancing your career while having the time of your life. Feel free to enrich both your personal and work life and hop on board!
  

  
**Why you'll love this job**
  

  
The Analyst/ Sr Analyst, Industrial Engineer is part of the Technical Operations Division. This role is responsible for analyzing and optimizing warehouse operations through the application of engineering principles, data analysis, and process improvement methodologies. By partnering with cross-functional teams, the role drives efficiency, improves labor and space utilization, and supports the integration of technology to enhance throughput, safety, and service performance.
  

  
**What you'll do**
  

  
_As noted above, this list is intended to reflect the current job but there may be additional essential functions (and certainly non-essential job functions) that are not referenced.  Management will modify the job or require other tasks be performed whenever it is deemed appropriate to do so, observing, of course, any legal obligations including any collective bargaining obligations._
  

  
•    Analyze warehouse workflows, layouts, and operational processes to identify and implement optimization opportunities that enhance efficiency and reduce waste
  
•    Develop and execute strategies to improve throughput, labor productivity, and space utilization in alignment with business objectives
  
•    Apply Engineering principles, techniques, and procedures, such statistical methods or mathematical calculations, to support the design, analysis, and optimization of systems, components, or processes
  
•    Conduct time studies, capacity assessments, and labor planning to support operational decision-making and resource allocation
  
•    Design scalable staffing models that align with current operational needs, projected growth, and service level expectations
  
•    Use qualitative and quantitative observations/ data to develop insights, inform decision-making, and support engineering analysis and problem-solving
  
•    Collaborate with Operations and Safety teams to develop ergonomic, safe, and efficient workstations and material handling processes
  
•    Lead continuous improvement initiatives targeting key performance indicators, including dock-to-stock time, stock-to-dock cycles, and service level compliance
  
•    Support the integration of warehouse technologies such as WMS, automation systems, and material handling equipment to enhance performance and accuracy
  
•    Translate operational data into actionable insights through dashboards, reporting tools, and performance visualizations that guide strategic decisions
  

  
**All you'll need for success**
  

  
**Minimum Qualifications- Education & Prior Job Experience**
  

  
•    Bachelor's Degree in Industrial Engineering, Operations Research, or related field
  
•    3+ years of experience in industrial engineering, operations research, or related field
  
•    Proven track record of driving process improvements and cost savings in a warehouse or manufacturing environment
  

  
**Preferred Qualifications- Education & Prior Job Experience**

Project focus would be mission-critical data center projects. This opportunity hires directly into that team and into an industry of more than $17 billion. Data Centers are an aggressive market for Burns & McDonnell and are highlighted by several priority articles on our site.
  
 
  

  
 
  
The Assistant Project Manager (APM) will assist project managers on a variety of Mission Critical projects of varying complexity, and/or will manage some smaller and less complex projects on their own. This position will manage and act as a coordinator and project liaison to the client, subcontractors, vendors, and other stakeholders, throughout the project. The APM will maintain document control, manage databases and coordinate and track project activities and communications to ensure project goals are accomplished within the prescribed timeframe and budget parameters.

title: Sr Operations Research Scientist, Operations Modeling and Analytics
description_text:
**Intro**
  

  
Are you ready to explore a world of possibilities, both at work and during your time off? Join our American Airlines family, and you’ll travel the world, grow your expertise and become the best version of you. As you embark on a new journey, you’ll tackle challenges with flexibility and grace, learning new skills and advancing your career while having the time of your life. Feel free to enrich both your personal and work life and hop on board!
  

  
**Why you'll love this job**
  

  
+ This position is part of the  **Operations Performance and Analytics Department**
  
+ This position serves as a key resource for analytical and decision support initiatives.
  
+ This position will also be instrumental in maintaining a strong and effective partnership with Ops Planning and Network Planning teams, The Hub Control Center, IOC, and Corporate Real Estate (CRE)
  
+ You will play a critical role in  **developing simulation-based solutions**  to optimize operational performance and support strategic decision-making across the airline
  

  
**What you'll do**
  

  
+  **Lead the development and enhancement of simulation models**  to evaluate operational scenarios, assess schedule robustness, and improve efficiency across airport, airspace, and ATC environments.
  
+ Build and maintain  **discrete-event and agent-based simulation frameworks**  using tools such as  **AirTOP, SIMMOD, Arena, SIMIO** , or similar platforms.
  
+ Design and implement  **simulation experiments**  to test operational strategies, quantify risk, and identify bottlenecks under varying conditions.
  
+ Collaborate with Ops Planning and Network Planning teams to  **simulate flight schedules**  and assess impacts on reliability and resource utilization.
  
+ Integrate simulation outputs with  **predictive analytics and optimization models**  to provide holistic decision support.
  
+ Communicate simulation findings effectively to stakeholders, translating complex results into actionable insights for operational and strategic planning.
  
+ Stay current with  **advances in simulation methodologies** , including stochastic modeling, Monte Carlo techniques, and hybrid simulation approaches.
  
+ Builds and maintain decision support models using  **simulation and/or statistical techniques**
  
+ Assists with the flight schedule development, design and evaluation process by developing quantitative tools that address operational efficiency and robustness
  
+ Supports the company's long term strategic and growth initiatives through simulation modeling and other related analysis
  
+ Communicates effectively with stakeholders across the company and promote data-driven decision making
  
+ Interfaces with IT departments, related business departments, and outside vendors to ensure coordinated planning, evaluation and implementation of new decision support systems
  
+ Research current practices and industry trends, generate options for improvement, test new ideas and help the business unit with implementation. Benchmark American Airlines' performance versus other airlines and industries.
  

  
**All you'll need for success**
  

  
**Minimum Qualifications- Education & Prior Job Experience**
  

  
+ Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative discipline (Operations Research, Industrial Engineering, Data Science, Applied Statistics, or related field).
  
+ 3+ years of experience in  **simulation modeling and analysis** .
  
+ Proficiency in building simulation models using  **AirTOP, SIMMOD, Arena, SIMIO** , or similar tools.
  
+ Strong programming skills in  **Python, R, or Java**  for simulation automation and data integration.
  
+ Experience with extraction, cleansing and analysis of large datasets to identify key trends and insights
  
+ Experience designing  **simulation experiments** , analyzing outputs, and validating models against real-world data.
  
+ Familiarity with  **optimization techniques**  (LP, MIP) and ability to integrate simulation with optimization frameworks.
  
+ Knowledge of  **data visualization tools**  (Tableau or similar) for presenting simulation results.
  
+ Excellent interpersonal, relational, and communication skills (both oral and written).
  

  
**Preferred Qualifications- Education & Prior Job Experience**
  

  
+ Domain knowledge in airline operations or transportation systems.
  
+ Experience with  **Big Data integration**  for simulation input and calibration.
  
+ Exposure to  **machine learning techniques**  for enhancing simulation accuracy and predictive capability.
  

  
**Skills, Licenses & Certifications**
  

  
+ Strong analytical mindset with a passion for  **simulation-driven problem solving** .
  
+ Ability to manage multiple simulation projects, prioritize tasks, and adapt to changing operational needs.
  
+ Excellent communication skills to convey technical insights to non-technical audiences.

title: Sales Support Team Lead
description_text:
**About the Role:**
  
As a CBRE Sales Support Lead, you will maximize the efficiency and delivery of services to clients by planning, scheduling, and overseeing the marketing and sales support functions for a team.
  

  
This job is part of the Sales Operations. They are responsible for sales planning, strategy, and forecasting to achieve business objectives.
  

  
**What You’ll Do:**
  

  
+ Plan, coordinate, schedule, and oversee the activities of the team members.
  
+ Review voucher forms and process Brokers' commission payments.
  
+ Maximize the sales team's productivity through review, evaluation, and implementation of effective business processes.
  
+ Oversee the tracking of office transactions (listings, sales/leases, expenses, client reimbursements, etc.).
  
+ Interact with clients on property tours and resolve issues and inquiries.
  
+ Consult with sales professionals and provide recommendations on marketing practices.
  
+ Coordinate the production and execution of property marketing campaigns that include property information materials, comparable market analyses, and targeted mailing lists.
  
+ Serve as a point of contact for the sales team for information requests.
  
+ Provide input for staff member’s performance review. Provide feedback and recommend recruitment, advancement, corrective action, and termination of employees.  Mentor, train, and coach junior sales team members to further development.
  
+ Resolve complex problems and identify appropriate approaches to existing solutions to accomplish team objectives.
  
+ May establish new techniques to ensure the team is able to meet its objectives.
  
+ Have a direct impact on the team objectives as well as the objectives of related teams.
  
+ Ensure personal and team outcomes have a positive impact on customer objectives.
  
+ Lead by example and model behaviors that are consistent with CBRE RISE values.  Guide team through the application of basic knowledge of practices and procedures.  Work to build consensus.
  

  
**What You’ll Need:**
  

  
+ Real Estate Salesperson license required
  
+ High School Diploma or GED with 5+ years of job-related experience. Prior shift manager or supervisory experience preferred. .
  
+ In-depth understanding of a range of processes, procedures, systems, and concepts within own job function and basic knowledge of related job functions required.
  
+ Requires the ability to explain complex concepts or sensitive information.
  
+ Expert knowledge of Microsoft Office products. Examples include Word, Excel, Outlook, etc. Internet research and web publishing skills.
  
+ Excellent organizational skills with a master-level inquisitive mindset.
  
+ Exceptional math skills. Ability to calculate sophisticated figures such as percentages, discounts, and markups.

title: Sr. L&D Specialist – Instructional Designer
description_text:
About the Role:
  
As a CBRE Sr. Learning and Development Specialist - Instructional Designer, you will partner closely with business units to assess learner needs and recommend scalable, sustainable trainings solutions that support business priorities. This role is responsible for designing, developing, and implementing high-quality learning programs using a mix of instructor-led, virtual, digital and self-directed learning approaches. The role applies instructional design methodologies, learning technologies, and microlearning strategies to create engaging and scalable learning solutions that support business outcomes.
  
What You’ll Do:
  
· Consults with business units and subject matter experts to identify learning needs and make recommendations for appropriate solutions that meet business demands.
  
· Designs and administers blended training programs and digital learning resources including eLearnings, videos and microlearnings and performance support materials using instructional design principles. Coordinates with stakeholders to ensure effective outcomes.
  
· Applies instructional design principles and adult learning theory to create engaging, effective and scalable learning experiences.
  
· Evaluates and leverages learning technologies and emerging digital tools to enhance content development, learner engagement, and the delivery of innovative learning solutions.
  
· Coordinates, designs and supports the delivery of virtual and in-person instructor-led training across the US.
  
· Creates and applies evaluation and assessment tools to measure learning effectiveness, impact and alignment with business goals.
  
· Applies instructional design principles to translate complex concepts into clear, practical learning experiences tailored to the needs of diverse audiences and job roles.
  
· Review training metrics, feedback and evaluation data to identify trends and drive continuous improvement of learning solutions.
  
· Manages communication and marketing plans for new courses and training programs.
  
· Remain current on learning industry trends, methodologies, and best practices and apply them effectively to program design and delivery.
  
· Develops sound relationships with business partners, operations, managers, consultants and trainers.
  
· Lead by example and model behaviors that are consistent with CBRE RISE values.
  
· Contribute to team success through effective communication and collaboration with teammates and partner team members.
  
· Solicit and incorporate feedback from participants, peers, and leaders to strengthen learning solutions and professional practice.
  
What You’ll Need:
  
· Bachelor's Degree preferred with 2-5 years of experience in learning and development roles, training, instructional design or related roles. In lieu of a degree, a combination of experience and education will be considered.
  
· In lieu of a degree, a combination of experience and education will be considered.
  
· Experience applying instructional design methodologies and adult learning principles.
  
· Experience designing digital learning content including eLearning, videos, microlearning and blended learning solutions.
  
· Experience delivering and facilitating training in-person and/or virtually.
  
· Excellent organization and project skills to coordinate program efforts.
  
· Ability to analyze possible solutions using technical experience to apply appropriate judgment and precedents.
  
· In-depth knowledge of Microsoft Office products. Examples include Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.
  
· Experience with learning management systems (LMS), such as Cornerstone OnDemand (CSOD).
  
· Experience with utilizing multimedia authoring tools such as Adobe Captivate, Articulate Storyline, Rise 360, Vyond and other emerging technologies to design online content.
  
CBRE carefully considers multiple factors to determine compensation, including a candidate’s education, training, and experience. title: Business Development Manager - Heavy Civil Engineering & Construction
description_text:
**Description** Join an established team of professional builders, engineers, and consultants in the North Texas Region, based out of the Dallas, Fort Worth, Austin, and Houston offices, to further expand a growing portfolio of Heavy Civil Construction work. As revenues have doubled in the last 2 years, Burns & McDonnell’s Integrated Design-Build Team is looking to add great talent that will continue to fill the pipeline of safe, successful, and profitable projects. This position will report directly to executive leadership in the North Texas Region.
  
 
  

  
 
  
The Business Development Manager is responsible for the overall management of direct award or competitive collaborative delivery (Civil Engineering, Design-Build, Progressive Design-Build, Construction Manager At-Risk) heavy civil project pursuits for private business, developers, and industrial clients as well as select Heavy Civil public sector markets. The Business Development Manager will be client facing to identify, negotiate and close deals with the strong support of an established civil engineering and civil construction team.
  
 
  

  
 
  
Beginning with an established network and customer relationships, the Business Development Manager will identify opportunities with customers and lead the team from the direct award or request-for-proposals through the contract close and early stages of project development, after which project supervision will transfer to the Project Execution team. The Business Development Manager will closely engage with the established engineering and construction execution teams and the individual project managers to create, facilitate and manage consistent and coherent project pursuits and startup process.
  
 
  

  
 
  
Key Responsibilities
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Establish, develop and maintain the sales engine for Heavy Civil Engineering and Construction in the North Texas Region and surrounding regions, with discretion afforded to specific pursuits across Texas and Nationwide. Pursuit examples may include mass grading, site utilities, potable water and sanitary sewer transmission and process elements, stormwater infrastructure, bridges, cast-in-place or pre-cast structures, paving, or other elements typically associated with Heavy Civil Construction.
  
 
  
+ Develop and maintain relationships with preferred construction execution partners aligned with Burns & McDonnell’s safety, financial, and execution culture.
  
 
  
+ Collaborate with North Texas Region Business Development Managers in adjacent business units on strategic pursuits and clients for Power, Industrial, Mission Critical, Vertical, and Process facilities.
  
 
  
+ Provide Heavy Civil deal flow support to adjacent business units.
  
 
  
+ Identify potential business deals by educating new and existing customers on collaborative delivery approaches, identifying strategic teaming agreements, and shaping project development to successfully win new work.
  
 
  
+ Screen potential business deals by analyzing commercial feasibility, deal requirements, and risk-adjusted return.
  
 
  
+ Drive pursuit strategy and preconstruction feasibility by leveraging robust understanding of heavy civil preconstruction processes and partnering with existing preconstruction and estimating teams to
  
 
  
+ Close new business deals by coordinating requirements, developing and negotiating contracts, and integrating contract requirements with business operations.
  
 
  
+ Oversee technical proposal development including scope, budget, schedule and resources. Direct and oversee management of technical proposal resources, in partnership with the pursuit manager.
  
 
  
+ Manage client-focused strategies and action plans that respond to client challenges and create a competitive advantage for the firm to successfully obtain the work.
  
 
  
+ Develop technical staff to pursue and capture projects including guiding development of technical and cost proposals and presentations.
  
 
  
+ Analyze project-specific risks and contribute to internal risk management processes lead by project and Executive level leadership.
  
 
  
+ Lead go/no-go analyses and decisions.
  
 
  
+ Facilitate and lead or oversee contract reviews and negotiations. Collaborate with marketing and executive leadership to review and approve all budgets and schedules for proposals efforts, including determining commercial strategy and pursuit profitability.
  
 
  
+ Work with department and project management to ensure that staff resource assignments are aligned with the project requirements with respect to roles, skills and quantity.
  
 
  
+ Lead client facing engagement and partner with Management to support overall client satisfaction.
  
 
  
+ Monitor delivery progress through engagement with project management to understand and respond to successes and opportunities for improvement in current and future projects.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Qualifications**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Bachelor’s degree in Construction Management, Engineering, or a related field with a minimum of 10 years of related professional experience. Or,
  
 
  
+ Associate’s degree in a related field with a minimum of 12 years of related professional experience. Or,
  
 
  
+ High school diploma or GED with a minimum of 14 years of related professional experience.
  
 
  
+ Proven experience in executing collaborative delivery projects required.
  
 
  
+ Expert experience in client and business development and proposal development.
  
 
  
+ Expert experience in project management including planning, design and construction team coordination and communication, budgeting, schedule management, and client management.
  
 
  
+ Experience in contract negotiations.
  
 
  
+ Demonstrated financial and client success on completed projects.
  
 
  
+ Ability to thoughtfully and positively influence, lead and manage.
  
 
  
+ Excellent facilitation, collaboration, organization, and problem-solving skills.
  
 
  
+ Excellent planning and analytical skills.
  
 
  
+ Excellent oral and written communication skills, and able to clearly and effectively present complex information to all levels of employees, management, and clients.
  
 
  
+ Strong interpersonal skills.
  
 
  
+ Proven industry knowledge is desired.
  
 
  
+ A Professional Engineer license is optional.
  
 
  

  
 
  
This job posting will remain open a minimum of 72 hours and on an ongoing basis until filled.
  
 
  

  
 
  
EEO/Disabled/Veterans
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Job** Business Development/Sales
  
**Primary Location** US-TX-Fort Worth
  
**Other Locations** United States
  
**Schedule:** Full-time
  
**Travel:** Yes, 50 % of the Time
  
**Req ID:** 263182

title: Commercial Real Estate Appraisal Officer II
description_text:
Why Bank OZK
  

  

  
 Founded on a legacy of more than 120 years in banking, Bank OZK is much more than just a company. We’re nationally recognized as an industry leader in financial services. That means we combine exceptional service with innovative technologies to deliver smart solutions to our clients across the country. We’re investing in small businesses, fueling economies in local communities and changing skylines in the largest cities across America. Here, we're not simply filling roles. We're fostering even greater careers. 
  

  
 
  

  
 The foundation for a great career starts with an exceptional team and a comprehensive benefits package. We believe in providing our dedicated team members with the best resources to support their physical, mental and financial wellbeing, including generous PTO, 401(k) matching, health, dental, vision (and pet!) insurance as well as special perks and discounts. Learn more about Bank OZK benefits (https://careers.ozk.com/benefits) . 
  

  
 
  
 
  
Job Purpose & Scope
  

  

  
 Manages a pipeline of moderately complex valuation projects across all property types.  In this role, you will scope valuation projects, engage external appraisers, and review appraisal reports to ensure our originations and special asset management teams have credible valuation reports and reliable collateral values for decision-making purposes.    
  
 
  
Essential Job Functions
  

  

  

  
+  Communicates with originations and credit personnel to understand the financing request and collateral property characteristics for appraisal scoping purposes.
  
+ Consults with first line personnel and develops solutions to appraisal problems.
  
+ Identifies qualified professionals from the appraiser panel to bid, award, and track appraisal projects through the Real Estate Information Management System (RIMS).
  
+ Manages the appraiser engagement process and collaborates with the commercial appraisal coordinator to ensure contracted appraisers have received all required property information.
  
+ Performs reviews on moderately complex appraisal projects to determine the credibility of the appraisal report and the reliability of the appraiser’s market value opinions.
  
+ Completes or reviews property evaluations and validations as needed.
  
+ Ensures appraisal reports comply with internal policies and procedures, engagement letter terms, and federal and state regulatory requirements including, but not limited to, USPAP and FIRREA.
  
+ Gathers and analyzes market data and trends that are relevant to appraisal engagements.
  
+ Provides insight and technical interpretations of real estate market conditions and appraisal theory to commercial real estate lending teams.
  
+ Mentors first and second line personnel in Bank appraisal policies, procedures, and practices.
  
+ Exercises discretion and judgment in the performance of essential job functions.
  
+ Maintains good punctuality and attendance to work.
  
+ Follows Bank policy, procedures, and guidelines. 
  

  

  
 
  
Knowledge, Skills & Abilities
  

  

  

  
+  Knowledge of commercial real estate appraisal standards and requirements applicable to federally regulated real estate transactions.
  
+ Knowledge of trending appraisal methods and appraisal review theory.
  
+ Knowledge of RIMS.
  
+ Ability to use a commercial appraisal management platform to manage your project pipeline.
  
+ Ability to use Argus.
  
+ Ability to communicate effectively both verbally and in writing.
  
+ Ability to think critically and consistently demonstrate good organizational skills.
  
+ Ability to demonstrate analytical skills including discounted cash flow analysis.
  
+ Ability to work with minimal direct supervision.
  
+ Ability to work effectively and demonstrate flexibility in a continually changing environment.
  
+ Ability to manage multiple tasks with exacting deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
  
+ Ability to maintain attention to detail.
  
+ Skill in computer and Microsoft Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Adobe Acrobat software. 
  

  

  
 
  
Basic Qualifications
  

  

  

  
+  High school diploma or commensurate work experience, required.
  
+ Bachelor’s degree, preferred.
  
+ Minimum of four (4) years’ commercial real estate valuation work experience, required, commercial real estate appraisal review with a bank, preferred.
  
+ State Certified General Appraiser designation, required.
  
+ AI-GRS or MAI designation, preferred.
  
+ Membership/designation in a professional appraisal association/society, preferred. 
  

  

  
 
  
Job Expectations
  

  

  
 Operate customary equipment and technology used in a business environment, with or without accommodation. 
  

  
 
  

  
 Note: This description is not an exhaustive list of all job functions, duties, skills, and job standards required.  Other job functions, duties, skills, and standards may be added.  Management reserves the right to add or change the job requirements at any time. 
  

  
 
  

  
 Position can also be also located in these locations: Raleigh, North Carolina; Atlanta, Georgia; Tampa, Florida; Little Rock, Arkansas; Miami, Florida; Houston, Texas; Charlotte, North Carolina 
  

  
#LI-KCI
  
 
  
EEO Statement
  

  

  
 Bank OZK is an equal opportunity employer and give consideration for employment to qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, and local law. Member FDIC.

title: Innovation & Technology Manager (OGC)
description_text:
**Description**
  
 
  
The Innovation & Technology Manager helps the Burns & McDonnell India OGC Global Practice turn technology, automation, data and digital delivery into better project execution. This is a practical transformation role: part manager, part facilitator and part delivery partner. The role manages the Innovation & Technology Group, reports directly to the Global Practice Manager, and works closely with the U.S. Innovation Director, U.S. technology counterparts, engineering & design teams, project teams and other Global Practices to convert strong ideas into measurable business outcomes. The role serves as AI Champion for the Global Practice, helps establish the OGC governance framework for responsible use of intelligent tools and automation, and guides selected initiatives through proof of concept, testing, rollout and adoption. The successful candidate may come from an engineering, design technology or project delivery background and should bring the judgment, leadership aptitude and passion for organizational transformation needed to stretch into a role that helps teams deliver cleaner work, reduce rework and move confidently into new ways of working.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Primary Responsibilities**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Manage the Innovation & Technology Group by setting priorities, coordinating resources, developing team capability and aligning activities with OGC business strategy.
  
 
  
+ Collaborate closely with the Global Practice Manager, U.S. Innovation Director, U.S. technology counterparts, engineering teams and project teams to deliver practical innovation outcomes.
  
 
  
+ Serve as the AI Champion for the Global Practice by promoting responsible, measurable use cases that improve quality, productivity, knowledge reuse and delivery consistency.
  
 
  
+ Help establish and support the OGC governance framework for intelligent tools and automation, including use-case intake, prioritization, risk review, data stewardship, adoption readiness and value tracking.
  
 
  
+ Support OGC disciplines in developing practical playbooks, identifying high-value automation opportunities, and conducting workshops, demonstrations and adoption sessions as required.
  
 
  
+ Act as project manager for multiple small technology and innovation initiatives moving through proof of concept, testing, pilot, rollout and adoption.
  
 
  
+ Partner with engineering and project teams to apply automation, digital tools and data-enabled workflows that improve deliverable completeness, precision and quality.
  
 
  
+ Focus technology efforts on measurable execution outcomes, including reduced comments, reduced rework and churn, better design maturity, stronger knowledge reuse and cleaner final deliverables.
  
 
  
+ Develop and maintain a Digital Shelf of reusable digital assets, automations, applications, dashboards, templates, delivery accelerators, lessons learned and drive a client-facing Digital Shelf concept.
  
 
  
+ Support execution-improvement initiatives such as deliverable health monitoring, checklist centralization, quality feedback loops and discipline-facing dashboards.
  
 
  
+ Collaborate with other Global Practices to help define future digital deliverables and technology-enabled client offerings.
  
 
  
+ Apply basic change management practices, including ADKAR awareness, stakeholder engagement, adoption planning, communication and reinforcement of new ways of working.
  
 
  
+ Apply Scrum principles where appropriate, including backlog development, prioritization, sprint planning, reviews and iterative product development.
  
 
  
+ Oversee software, systems, tools and technology support to ensure effective utilization, escalation, troubleshooting, ticket management, cybersecurity and data governance compliance.
  
 
  
+ Recruit, develop, mentor and retain a growing team of developers, data engineers and technology specialists working closely with U.S. counterparts.
  
 
  
+ Provide performance evaluation input for Technology & Innovation Group members, sub-teams and design application specialists.
  
 
  
+ Perform other assigned duties.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Qualifications**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Bachelor's degree in engineering, architecture, construction, or a related field from an accredited program.
  
 
  
+ At least 10–15 years of professional experience in engineering, design and/or project engineering or project management.
  
 
  
+ Prior leadership or management experience.
  
 
  
+ Strong interest in engineering design technologies, for example Hexagon/SmartPlant.
  
 
  
+ Interest in automation, innovation, integration, digitalization, and digitization.
  
 
  
+ Excellent oral and written communication skills, strong interpersonal skills, and the ability to present complex information clearly.
  
 
  
+ Strong collaboration, organization, analytical, and problem-solving skills.
  
 
  
+ Proven ability to positively influence, lead, and manage change.
  
 
  
+ Strong project management skills and strategic perspective.
  
 
  
+ Advanced knowledge of technology and its application in business.
  
 
  
+ Proven success in project leadership, planning, and budgeting.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Additional Requirements**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Ability to collect and analyze information to make informed decisions. Must exercise sound judgment, solve problems, organize and prioritize responsibilities, read and apply financial and quantitative analysis, speak confidently in front of others, and work under stressful situations. Excellent cognitive skills, attention to detail, and the ability to multitask are necessary. Must understand and apply written/verbal instructions and be capable of delegating instructions.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Travel**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Annual travel to the U.S. is required to attend the Hexagon Conference and other industry summits, participate in strategic planning and collaborate with the U.S. Innovation Director and technology leadership counterparts. Must possess a U.S. visa or demonstrate eligibility for a U.S. visa.
  
 
  

  
 
  
This job posting will remain open a minimum of 72 hours and on an ongoing basis until filled.

title: Systems Administrator
description_text:
Why Bank OZK
  

  

  
 Founded on a legacy of more than 120 years in banking, Bank OZK is much more than just a company. We’re nationally recognized as an industry leader in financial services. That means we combine exceptional service with innovative technologies to deliver smart solutions to our clients across the country. We’re investing in small businesses, fueling economies in local communities and changing skylines in the largest cities across America. Here, we're not simply filling roles. We're fostering even greater careers. 
  

  
 
  

  
 The foundation for a great career starts with an exceptional team and a comprehensive benefits package. We believe in providing our dedicated team members with the best resources to support their physical, mental and financial wellbeing, including generous PTO, 401(k) matching, health, dental, vision (and pet!) insurance as well as special perks and discounts. Learn more about Bank OZK benefits (https://careers.ozk.com/benefits) . 
  

  
 
  
 
  
Job Purpose & Scope
  

  

  
 The scope of this job will include administration of the Bank’s IT infrastructure, including security, performance, and availability. This position will perform highly complex projects involving the design, analysis, maintenance, and implementation of the IT infrastructure. Responsibilities also include ensuring data and system security by protecting corporate information from unauthorized access, use or interruption; eliminating bottlenecks in the network; and identifying and resolving virtual hardware and software problems. 
  

  
 
  
 
  
Essential Job Functions
  
+ Maintains the bank’s IT infrastructure, including security, performance and availability as it pertains to VMware virtual environments, Microsoft Active Directory/Exchange and Microsoft SCCM.
  
+ Participates in IT projects involving the design, analysis, maintenance and implementation of IT infrastructure containing VMware virtual environments, Microsoft Active Directory/Exchange and Microsoft SCCM elements.
  
+ Ensures data and system security by protecting corporate information from unauthorized access, use or interruption by:
  
+ Research and recommend security measures to be included in the installation of new systems.
  
+ Monitor and evaluate data security implemented in the system; work to reduce all risks to corporate computer resources.
  
+ Ensure that the security program provides the necessary safeguards for good business practice and that it meets all corporate and government regulatory requirements.
  
+ Eliminates bottlenecks in the data workflow from applications to the physical/virtual network.
  
+ Identifies and resolves virtual hardware and software problems.
  
+ Coordinates software upgrades with vendors and bank staff as necessary.
  
+ Assists in planning and analyze information concerning job-processing time, virtual hardware/software utilization, CPU loads, system tuning, hardware tuning and virtual hardware integration.
  
+ Performs installation, configuration, and management of all virtual servers and appliances.
  
+ Performs upgrades Microsoft environments such as Active Directory, Exchange, and SCCM.
  
+ Assists in maintenance for VMware environment. 
  

  

  
+  Regularly assists the help desk with elevated tickets.
  
+ Follows Bank and department policy, practice and procedure while maintaining strict confidentiality and bank secrecy.
  
+ Establishes and maintains working relationships with end users, vendors, and managers.
  
+ Drives an automobile on company business.
  
+ Maintains nightly server backups.
  
+ Regularly exercises discretion and judgment in the performance of other essential job functions.
  
+ Maintains good punctuality and attendance to work. 
  

  

  

  
 
  

  
 
  
 
  
Knowledge, Skills & Abilities
  
+ Knowledge of storage hardware, physical server hardware, and software installation, along with testing and operations.
  
+ Knowledge of VMware and basic fundamental virtual hypervisors.
  
+ Knowledge of Microsoft Windows Server software.
  
+ Knowledge of local area networks and fundamental concepts.
  
+ Knowledge of the principles of data communications.
  
+ Knowledge of various software products such as Microsoft Office, Microsoft RDP, Microsoft SQL, Microsoft SCCM.
  
+ Knowledge of Microsoft PowerShell, and VMware power CLI.
  
+ Ability to work without close supervision.
  
+ Excellent customer service skills.
  
+ Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  
+ Ability to work extended hours as necessary. 
  

  

  

  
 
  

  
 
  
 
  
Basic Qualifications
  
+ Bachelor’s degree in job-related field or commensurate work experience, required.
  
+ 3+ years of work experience with the software and systems referenced above, required.
  
+ Microsoft or IT certification, preferred. 
  

  

  

  
 
  
 
  
Job Expectations
  

  

  
 Operate customary equipment and technology used in a business environment, with or without accommodation.

title: Sr. L&D Specialist – Instructional Designer
description_text:

As a CBRE Sr. Learning and Development Specialist - Instructional Designer, you will partner closely with business units to assess learner needs and recommend scalable, sustainable trainings solutions that support business priorities. This role is responsible for designing, developing, and implementing high-quality learning programs using a mix of instructor-led, virtual, digital and self-directed learning approaches. The role applies instructional design methodologies, learning technologies, and microlearning strategies to create engaging and scalable learning solutions that support business outcomes.
  
What You’ll Do:
  
· Consults with business units and subject matter experts to identify learning needs and make recommendations for appropriate solutions that meet business demands.
  
· Designs and administers blended training programs and digital learning resources including eLearnings, videos and microlearnings and performance support materials using instructional design principles. Coordinates with stakeholders to ensure effective outcomes.
  
· Applies instructional design principles and adult learning theory to create engaging, effective and scalable learning experiences.
  
· Evaluates and leverages learning technologies and emerging digital tools to enhance content development, learner engagement, and the delivery of innovative learning solutions.
  
· Coordinates, designs and supports the delivery of virtual and in-person instructor-led training across the US.
  
· Creates and applies evaluation and assessment tools to measure learning effectiveness, impact and alignment with business goals.
  
· Applies instructional design principles to translate complex concepts into clear, practical learning experiences tailored to the needs of diverse audiences and job roles.
  
· Review training metrics, feedback and evaluation data to identify trends and drive continuous improvement of learning solutions.
  
· Manages communication and marketing plans for new courses and training programs.
  
· Remain current on learning industry trends, methodologies, and best practices and apply them effectively to program design and delivery.
  
· Develops sound relationships with business partners, operations, managers, consultants and trainers.
  
· Lead by example and model behaviors that are consistent with CBRE RISE values.
  
· Contribute to team success through effective communication and collaboration with teammates and partner team members.
  
· Solicit and incorporate feedback from participants, peers, and leaders to strengthen learning solutions and professional practice.
  
What You’ll Need:
  
· Bachelor's Degree preferred with 2-5 years of experience in learning and development roles, training, instructional design or related roles. In lieu of a degree, a combination of experience and education will be considered.
  
· In lieu of a degree, a combination of experience and education will be considered.
  
· Experience applying instructional design methodologies and adult learning principles.
  
· Experience designing digital learning content including eLearning, videos, microlearning and blended learning solutions.
  
· Experience delivering and facilitating training in-person and/or virtually.
  
· Excellent organization and project skills to coordinate program efforts.
  
· Ability to analyze possible solutions using technical experience to apply appropriate judgment and precedents.
  
· In-depth knowledge of Microsoft Office products. Examples include Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.
  
· Experience with learning management systems (LMS), such as Cornerstone OnDemand (CSOD).
  
· Experience with utilizing multimedia authoring tools such as Adobe Captivate, Articulate Storyline, Rise 360, Vyond and other emerging technologies to design online content.

title: Senior Site Safety & Health Specialist - Construction
description_text:
**Description**
  
 
  
The Senior Site Safety & Health Specialist is responsible for leading and implementing project specific safety policies and procedures and provide professional Safety & Health leadership to the project management team in a heavy construction environment.
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Administer the Safety & Health programs on field construction projects to verify operations are executed as planned and conducted in a safe manner.
  
 
  
+ Manage the Site Safety & Health program deliverables as defined by the Corporate Safety & Health Department of Burns & McDonnell while responding to questions and/or concerns related to site specific safety and health compliance issues in a timely manner.
  
 
  
+ Manage compliance with applicable Local, State, and Federal Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) rules and regulations.
  
 
  
+ Manage Safety & Health programs in order to achieve and maintain the company goal of zero safety incidents.
  
 
  
+ Conduct regular health and safety performance reviews, assessments and complete documentation to verify that locations are in compliance with Corporate and regulatory requirements.
  
 
  
+ Manage and resolve actions causing a work stoppage when circumstances could result in immediate danger to life and health on a project.
  
 
  
+ Report and present work stoppage issues to the project management team and collaborate with the team to develop practices to prevent future work stoppage situations.
  
 
  
+ Consult with site personnel on Burns & McDonnell's Safety & Health issues and recommend best practices.
  
 
  
+ Assess Safety & Health issues and opportunities at the project site, before the work begins and implement corrective action based on policies, guidelines and procedures for resolution.
  
 
  
+ Manage the on-site Safety & Health program directly with the client Safety & Health Department to ensure collaboration on any site safety & health issues, coordination of preventative measures, and building working relationships with project staff and each subcontractor safety representative to ensure proper compliance is maintained.
  
 
  
+ Manage project Safety & Health walk-through assessments. Review all assessment findings with appropriate parties. Follow up by documenting the items which require immediate correction and ensure that these items are corrected in a timely manner.
  
 
  
+ Compile memos relative to safety, including assisting Site Management with correspondence to heighten safety awareness, project safety and health goals and ensure a safe and productive work environment is a priority among all project personnel.
  
 
  
+ Lead, conduct, validate, and document training on Safety & Health subjects as applicable to the work being conducted.
  
 
  
+ Review near miss, property damage, environmental incidents and accident investigation reports. Recommend countermeasures as appropriate.
  
 
  
+ Recommend solutions to Corporate Safety & Health regarding the Project Safety & Health Program challenges, objectives, and progress; ensuring high level of communication.
  
 
  
+ Responsible for the completion of Burns & McDonnell Daily Field Safety & Health Report forms, maintain all required safety records in an audit ready state and submit all documentation as required to Corporate Safety & Health.
  
 
  
+ Drive to various project site locations.
  
 
  
+ Performs other duties as assigned.
  
 
  
+ Complies with all policies and standards.
  
 
  

  
 
  
**Qualifications**
  
 
  

  
 
  
+ Bachelor's degree in occupational health and safety, engineering, or construction management and 7 years construction safety, general industry safety, or construction management, required or
  
 
  
+ Associate degree in occupational health and safety, engineering, or construction management and 9 years construction safety, general industry safety, or construction management, required or
  
 
  
+ High School Diploma/GED and 11 years construction safety, general industry safety, or construction management, required.
  
 
  
+ Experienced knowledge of Occupational Safety & Health Administration (OSHA) Construction and General Industry Regulations, as well as America National Standards Institution (ANSI), National Institute for Occupational Safety & Health (NIOSH), Army Corps of Engineers (CoE), Engineers Manual 385-1 and National Electrical Code (NEC) Standards.
  
 
  
+ Must be proficient in the use of computer software (i.e., Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  
 
  
+ Must be willing to work various schedules at the project site location.
  
 
  
+ Must meet standards to qualify for and maintain the Company's vehicle driving privileges as outlined in the Company's Motor Vehicle Safety Policy.
  
 
  
+ Valid driver's license required.
  
 
  
+ OSHA 10-hour Construction Outreach Safety Training Course required or
  
 
  
+ OSHA 30-hour Construction Outreach Safety Training Course required.

description_text:
**Intro**
  

  
Are you ready to explore a world of possibilities, both at work and during your time off? Join our American Airlines family, and you’ll travel the world, grow your expertise and become the best version of you. As you embark on a new journey, you’ll tackle challenges with flexibility and grace, learning new skills and advancing your career while having the time of your life. Feel free to enrich both your personal and work life and hop on board!
  

  
**Why you'll love this job**
  

  
+ Fleet Service Clerks perform the handling of items on and off aircraft, carts, containers, and trucks; transporting items between terminals and aircraft; receiving, delivering, weighing and documenting of cargo in a cargo specific facility (warehouse) or a loading dock area.
  
+ The terms and conditions of this position are covered by the Transport Workers Union (TWU) Collective Bargaining Agreement. Starting pay is $20.63 per hour.
  

  
**What you'll do**
  

  
**These are the essential functions of the job**

+  Drive and operate belt loaders

+ Perform catering service

+ Good communication skills

+ Must have the ability to pass the US Postal Service background check
  

  
**What you'll get**
  

  
Feel free to take advantage of all that American Airlines has to offer:
  

  
+ Travel Perks: Ready to explore the world? You, your family and your friends can reach 365 destinations on more than 6,800 daily flights across our global network.
  
+ Health Benefits: On day one, you’ll have access to your health, dental, prescription and vision benefits to help you stay well. And that’s just the start, we also offer virtual doctor visits, flexible spending accounts and more.
  
+ Wellness Programs: We want you to be the best version of yourself – that’s why our wellness programs provide you with all the right tools, resources and support you need.
  
+ 401(k) Program: Available upon hire and, depending on the workgroup, employer contributions to your 401(k) program are available after one year.
  
+ Additional Benefits: Other great benefits include our Employee Assistance Program, pet insurance and discounts on hotels, cars, cruises and more
  

  
**Feel free to be yourself at American**
  

  
From the team members we hire to the customers we serve, inclusion and diversity are the foundation of the dynamic workforce at American Airlines. title: Learning & Development Consultant
description_text:
About the Role:
  
As a CBRE Learning and Development Consultant, you will assist in managing the development, implementation, and administration of scalable learning and development programs that support business objectives. You will partner with stakeholders to identify needs, recommend solutions, and drive learning initiatives that enhance employee capability, performance, and engagement.
  
What You’ll Do:
  
· Consults with business units and subject matter experts to identify learning and performance needs, recommend appropriate solutions and guide content development efforts.
  
· Designs and administers training programs (virtual and live instructor led trainings, digital learning resources, videos, eLearnings, and microlearnings) and recommends delivery approaches that best support learning outcomes and business needs.
  
· Applies learning and instructional design expertise to address complex business challenges and develop innovative learning solutions.
  
· Coordinates, designs and delivers virtual and in-person instructor-led training across the US.
  
· Creates and applies evaluation and assessment tools to measure learning effectiveness, impact and alignment with business goals.
  
· Translates complex concepts into clear, practical learning solutions that meet the needs of diverse audiences and support learning and performance goals.
  
· Review training metrics, feedback and evaluation data to identify trends and drive continuous improvement of learning solutions.
  
· Manages communication and marketing plans for new courses and training programs.
  
· Remain current on learning industry trends, methodologies, and best practices and apply them effectively to program design and delivery.
  
· Develops strong relationships and solicit and incorporate feedback from business partners, operations, managers, consultants and trainers.
  
· Lead by example and model behaviors that are consistent with CBRE RISE values.
  
· Contribute to team success through effective communication and collaboration with teammates and partner team members.
  
What You’ll Need:
  
· Bachelor's Degree preferred with 5-8 years of experience in learning and development roles, training, instructional design or related roles. In lieu of a degree, a combination of experience and education will be considered.
  
· Experience delivering and facilitating training in-person and/or virtually.
  
· Ability to work autonomously within broad guidelines and adapt to evolving business needs and priorities.
  
· Ability to exercise judgment based on the analysis of multiple sources of information.
  
· Willingness to take a new perspective on existing solutions.
  
· Excellent organization and project skills to coordinate program efforts.
  
· Experience with utilizing multimedia authoring tools such as Adobe Captivate, Articulate Storyline and Rise 360 and/or other emerging technologies to design online content preferred.
  
· In-depth knowledge of Microsoft Office products. Examples include Word, Excel, Outlook, etc.
  
CBRE carefully considers multiple factors to determine compensation, including a candidate’s education, training, and experience. The minimum salary for the Learning & Development Consultant position is $85,000 annually [or $40.86 per hour] and the maximum salary for the  Learning & Development Consultant position is $95,000 annually [or $45.67 per hour]. The compensation that is offered to a successful candidate will depend on the candidate’s skills, qualifications, and experience.
  
Equal Employment Opportunity: CBRE is an equal opportunity employer that values diversity. We have a long-standing commitment to providing equal employment opportunity to all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, veteran status, political belief, or any other basis protected by applicable law.
  
Candidate Accommodations: CBRE values the differences of all current and prospective employees and recognizes how every employee contributes to our company’s success. CBRE provides reasonable accommodations in job application procedures for individuals with disabilities. If you require assistance due to a disability in the application or recruitment process, please submit a request via email at recruitingaccommodations@cbre.com or via telephone at +1 866 225 3099 (U.S.) and +1 866 388 4346 (Canada).

title: Loan Officer, AACU Branch Operations
description_text:
